identitySentence: Law Offices of David W Biles, PC is a boutique Denton firm where attorney David W Biles has helped North Texas families with estate planning, probate, wills, and powers of attorney since 1987.

concept.headline: Wills, Probate, and Estate Planning From a Denton Attorney With Over 30 Years of Experience
concept.subheadline: Planning your estate or settling a loved one's affairs can feel overwhelming at an already emotional time. Attorney David W. Biles explains your rights and options in plain language so you can make informed decisions that protect your family and your assets.
credibilityPoints: Attorney David W. Biles, practicing law since 1984
Boutique practice focused on estate planning, probate, wills, and powers of attorney

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The firm concentrates on estate planning and probate, including wills, trusts, and powers of attorney, as well as representation for executors, administrators, and beneficiaries in probate proceedings and will contests. Family law remains part of the practice for individuals and families across North Texas.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The Law Offices of David W. Biles, P.C. has maintained a legal presence in Denton since 1987, serving clients across North Texas from its appointment-only Denton offices.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your consultation is a straightforward conversation about your situation, your family, and what you want your plan to accomplish, with video conferences available if visiting the office is difficult. You will leave understanding your options and the likely next steps, with no obligation to move forward.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the Law Offices of David W. Biles, P.C.:

Your firm has kept a legal presence in Denton since 1987, and attorney David W. Biles has practiced law since 1984. That kind of continuity is exactly what families look for when they need a will drafted or a loved one's estate probated.

Your website already does a good job of inviting visitors to request a consultation. What no website can do on its own is answer an estate planning or probate inquiry the moment it arrives at 9 p.m., recognize whether the writer needs a will, a power of attorney, or probate help, offer your real consultation times, and secure the appointment before the person moves on. That is the gap our Lead Capturer fills.

We have already built a custom landing page concept for your firm, anchored by this identity sentence written specifically for you: "Law Offices of David W Biles, PC is a boutique Denton firm where attorney David W Biles has helped North Texas families with estate planning, probate, wills, and powers of attorney since 1987." It comes with a custom contact form tailored to your practice areas.

Here is how it works when someone reaches out:

- The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox with every form field included.
- The potential client automatically receives a topical response specific to their message, so a probate question gets a probate-focused answer rather than a generic autoreply.
- The response presents your available consultation times.
- A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the time on your calendar is a committed one.

We would like you to judge it by evidence, not by our description. We offer a 15-day proof period during which you can test the complete experience end to end.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm your firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will address it.

If the full 15 days run cleanly, you will know exactly what the Lead Capturer does for your practice because you watched it happen. To see the demo built for your firm, simply reply to this email.

Respectfully,
The IntakeBrief Team

[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
